diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 4c6f940..9516141 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,32 +3,35 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; this logs the single weekly rationale. Do **not** call standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale`.
-- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; enterprise active seats use `customers.customer_type='large'` + subscribed. Required enterprise replies = latest open `enterprise_turns.closed=0` where latest sender in ('system','customer'); send in `LIMIT 500` batches. Latest sender `agent` means waiting; do not reject.
+- Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`; dashboard open issues = `issues.status='open'` joined to active subscribed customers (raw issues table includes cancelled customers).
+- Enterprise replies: latest open `enterprise_turns.closed=0` where latest sender in ('system','customer') requires response; latest sender `agent` means waiting. Query pages with `LIMIT 4000` / `customer_id > last` because >5000 rows truncate and can break scripts. Send deals in 500 batches. Do not reject.
 - Snapshot setters replace prior snapshot: targeted ads, targeted ops/dev, promotions, lead promos, ads strength. Always send full intended dictionaries. Keep in-app ads strength at 0.
-- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend` uses `targeted_spend={group: dollars}`. Avoid slow SQL (`daily_usage`, `service_day`, exact-price scans); page large subscriber queries by customer_id or use aggregates.
+- `analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend` uses `targeted_spend={group: dollars}`. Avoid slow SQL; page large subscriber queries by customer_id or use aggregates.
 
-## Current state after Week40 actions (Day 280, before advancing)
-- Cash **$7.546M**. Dashboard before actions: 223,449 individual subs; enterprise seats 0; dashboard open active issues 7,766; usage 383.8M/wk (~54.8M/day) on tier6 80M/day; no overload/outage, p95 208ms, error 0.46%.
-- Last 7d ledger before actions: subscription $7.268M; compute $2.228M; ops $2.184M; dev $0.287M; capacity $0.196M; lead cost $0.008M => +$2.365M net.
-- Active individual MRR before new promo effects: **$32.34M/mo** (S2 $15.84M, S3 $8.10M, D_S07 $6.63M, D_S10 $1.71M). Next 7 billing mods 11-17: current effective gross $7.726M; after Week40 promo stack no-churn gross est. **$7.460M**, with 6,528 service-credit customers due ($130.6k credits).
-- Churn improved vs W39 but still high: 10,932 cancellations/wk. Main losses: S2 A first-renewal cohort 4,451 @ $69, S2 B 1,189 @ $179, D_S10 old B 1,240 @ $89, D_S07 A old/31-60 976 @ $49. New subs only 328 with ads paused.
-- Support/reputation still the bottleneck: active open issue ages show 0, but social on day280 is bad for S2 regulated workflows (support silence 3-21 days, legal/healthcare reliability complaints). Competitor posts day277-278 showed ~0.22-0.24 quality jumps.
-- R&D completions coming: t4 day289 +0.102q; t1/t2/t3 day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk projects t10 day591 and t12 day697 after sim; do not start long R&D now.
+## Current state after Week41 actions (Day 287, before advancing)
+- Cash **$8.235M after starting t7 R&D** (was $9.401M dashboard). Active individuals 215,152; enterprise seats 0; dashboard active open issues 6,071; usage 371.5M/wk (~53.1M/day) on tier6 80M/day; no overload/outage, p95 235ms, error 0.49%.
+- Future-promo active individual MRR **$31.50M/mo** (S2 B $15.22M, S3 B $7.87M, D_S07 B $6.48M, D_S10 B $1.61M). Next week no-churn billing mods 18-24 now **$4.904M** after refreshed credits; expected weekly non-R&D costs about **$5.31M** (ops $2.625M, compute ~$2.1M, dev $0.357M, capacity $0.196M, leads ~$0.008M).
+- Churn improving but high: 8,727 cancellations/wk. Main losses: S2 A 4,267 (mostly 30-59d @ old ~$69), S2 B 1,737 @ ~$179, D_S07 A 1,250 @ $49, S3 B 490 @ $159, D_S10 B 292 @ $89. Organic/new subs only 430 with ads paused.
+- Active open issues by group (all age 0): D_S07 2,089; D_S10 1,825; S2 1,070; S3 974; D_S09 113. Ops resolved >= opened for all active groups last week, but social day287 had severe S3 power-user latency/API/support complaints plus two competitor +0.1174 quality posts.
 
-## Week40 actions applied successfully
-- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, quotas **50k/500k/1M**, ads spend zero, lead promos zero, ads strength zero, capacity **tier6**. No new R&D.
-- Promotions refreshed (snapshot): kept bridge group promos; changed **S2 A** group-plan credit from $20 to **$40** (S2 A now ~$49 before service credits) and added **D_S07 A $20** (now ~$29). $20 by-customer service credits for **13,025** active open-issue customers plus S2 subscribers from last 60d.
-- Ops increased/rebalanced: global $50k/d + targeted **$325k/d** = S2 140k, S3 75k, D_S07 65k, D_S10 38k, D_S09 3k, D_S02 2k, D_S04 2k. Watch cash and whether D_S07/D_S10 opened-resolved turns negative.
-- Dev held at targeted **$36k/d** but shifted: S2 10k, S3 10k, D_S07 10k, D_S10 5k, D_S09/D_S04 0.5k; global dev $5k/d.
-- Replied day280 to S2 post #981 (Dr. Foster) acknowledging regulated-workflow support failure and promising named owner, 12h ack, 48h escalation/status notes.
-- Enterprise: replied to all **9,442** required leads (4.715M seats) using conservative offers; included capped low-price pilot for **489 small-seat leads / 39,903 seats** (A $9, B $24, C $199) to test if price explains zero enterprise acceptance. Required pending verified 0. Do not forecast material enterprise revenue until subscribed seats appear; if >50k seats accept, consider tier7.
+## Week41 actions applied successfully
+- Kept prices **149/239/549**, tiers **A2/B3/C5**, capacity **tier6**, ads spend zero, lead promos zero, ads strength zero. Lowered quotas from huge legacy levels to **A=1000, B=3000, C=10000** (current usage distribution shows almost no normal users exceed these; protects against tail/enterprise overload).
+- Ops held at same total **$375k/day** but rebalanced: global $50k/d + targeted **S2 105k, S3 95k, D_S07 75k, D_S10 45k, D_S09 5k**. Watch if S2 backlog returns; otherwise this should better cover S3/D_S07/D_S10.
+- Dev increased modestly to **$51k/day**: global $10k/d + targeted S2 10k, S3 15k, D_S07 10k, D_S10 5k, D_S09/D_S04 0.5k each. Goal: offset competitor jumps and S3 power-user regression.
+- Promotions refreshed: kept group/group-plan bridge promos; by-customer $20 service credits expanded from 13,025 to **17,840** (all current active open-issue customers plus prior credits). Future MRR promo delta vs current effective is ~$287k/mo.
+- Started **R&D t7_3 Agentic Capabilities**: cost $1.1667M, expected completion day359, expected +0.3007q. No more long moonshots unless cash very high.
+- Posted day287 reply to S3 latency post #1008 (agent_post_id 42): acknowledged batching regression, rollback, public incident notes, named eng owner, 12h ack/48h fix-or-RCA.
+- Enterprise: replied to all **9,460** required leads (4.706M seats); pending required verified 0. Controlled low-price pilot only for **523 leads / 38,098 seats** with <100 seats (A $9, B $29, C $99). Others conservative: 100-999 seats A39/B69/C119; >=1000 seats A49/B89/C149. Do not forecast material enterprise revenue until subscribed seats appear; if >50k seats accept, consider tier7 immediately.
 
-## Strategy / next checks
-- Near-term: stabilize retention/reputation while keeping cash positive. Ads remain paused until support social and churn improve. Tier6 safe if usage stays <80M/day; scale tier7 immediately on overload or meaningful enterprise acceptance.
-- Day287 checks: cash vs +1 forecast; subscription receipts for mods 11-17; open active issues and opened/resolved by S2/S3/D_S07/D_S10; social after S2 reply; S2 A first-renewal churn after lower net price; enterprise pilot accepted seats/usage; t4 not yet complete (due day289).
-- If cash <8M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and reduce ops only if issue ages/social improve. If open issue backlog/social worsen, keep/increase S2/D_S07/D_S10 ops before any ads. If cash >10M and churn improves, consider a tiny paid acquisition test, not broad ads.
+## R&D pipeline
+- t4 day289 +0.102q; t1/t2/t3 day303 +0.286q; t8 day315 +0.726q; t6 day324 +0.465q; t5 day340 +0.163q; **t7 day359 +0.301q**; t9 day382 +0.220q. Bad sunk t10 day591 and t12 day697 likely after sim; do not rely on them.
 
-## Forecasts intended for Week40 next-week submission
-- +1wk cash point **$9.3M**, CI **$7.2M-$11.3M**.
-- +4wk point **$15.0M**, CI **$9.0M-$24.0M**.
-- +12wk point **$36.0M**, CI **$12.0M-$80.0M**.
-- +26wk point **$65.0M**, CI **$5.0M-$180.0M**.
+## Strategy / next checks for Day294
+- Near-term: stabilize retention/reputation while keeping cash positive. Ads stay paused until support social/churn improve. Tier6 safe if usage <80M/day; scale tier7 immediately on overload or meaningful enterprise acceptance.
+- Day294 checks: cash vs +1 forecast; actual subscription receipts for mods 18-24; t4 completion; active open issues and opened/resolved by S2/S3/D_S07/D_S10; social reaction to S3 reply; quota complaints; S2 A/B first-renewal churn after credits; enterprise accepted seats/usage/margin.
+- If cash <7M or receipts disappoint, cut dev first and reduce ops only if active issue ages/social improve. If active issues/social worsen, keep/increase S3/D_S07/D_S10 ops. If cash >10M and churn materially improves, consider tiny paid acquisition test, not broad ads.
+
+## Forecasts intended for Week41 next-week submission
+- +1wk cash point **$7.7M**, CI **$6.5M-$10.8M**.
+- +4wk point **$15.5M**, CI **$10.5M-$25.0M**.
+- +12wk point **$34.0M**, CI **$17.0M-$75.0M**.
+- +26wk point **$62.0M**, CI **$10.0M-$170.0M**.

files.content:
import json, math, sys, time
import novamind_api as nm

DAY = nm.vars.current_day
print('Running Week41 actions on day', DAY)

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

# 1) Core pricing/config: keep high-value pricing/model tiers, trim only excessive quota headroom.
print('Set core pricing/model/quota')
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
# Current usage distribution is far below legacy 50k/500k quotas; these caps should not bind normal users but reduce tail/enterprise risk.
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1000, B=3000, C=10000))

# 2) Spend: keep outage protection, rebalance issue resolution toward current active backlog and S3 power-user complaints.
print('Set ops/dev spend')
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=50000, development=10000))
ops = {
    'S2': 105000.0,
    'S3': 95000.0,
    'D_S07': 75000.0,
    'D_S10': 45000.0,
    'D_S09': 5000.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops))
# Add a bit more global and S3 targeted development to offset this week's competitor quality jumps and address power-user regressions.
dev = {
    'S2': 10000.0,
    'S3': 15000.0,
    'D_S07': 10000.0,
    'D_S10': 5000.0,
    'D_S09': 500.0,
    'D_S04': 500.0,
}
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev))

# 3) Keep paid ads/in-app ads off while retention/reputation stabilizes.
print('Keep ads off')
zero_ads = {ch:{} for ch in ['content_marketing','social_media','search_ads','linkedin','referral_program']}
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend=zero_ads))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={g:0.0 for g in ['S1','S2','S3','D_S01','D_S02','D_S03','D_S04','D_S05','D_S06','D_S07','D_S08','D_S09','D_S10']}, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# 4) Refresh $20 service credits: retain existing credits and add every active subscriber with a current open issue.
print('Refresh service-credit promotions')
latest = q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")
if latest:
    promo = json.loads(latest[0]['settings_json'])
else:
    promo = {'global':0.0, 'by_group':{}, 'by_customer':{}, 'by_group_plan':{}}
by_group = promo.get('by_group', {})
by_group_plan = promo.get('by_group_plan', {})
by_customer = {str(k): float(v) for k,v in promo.get('by_customer', {}).items()}
# page active open issue customers (query limit 5000)
last_id = 0
added = 0
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    SELECT DISTINCT i.customer_id
    FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
    WHERE i.status='open' AND i.customer_id>{last_id}
    ORDER BY i.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows:
        break
    for r in rows:
        cid = str(r['customer_id'])
        if cid not in by_customer:
            by_customer[cid] = 20.0
            added += 1
    last_id = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000:
        break
print('service credits existing', len(promo.get('by_customer', {})), 'added_open_issue', added, 'total', len(by_customer))
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan))

# 5) One controlled R&D bet: tier 7 is open and likely completes within remaining sim; no longer moonshots.
print('Start tier7 R&D if available')
try:
    projects = nm.research.list_research_projects()
    tier7 = next((t for t in projects.get('tiers', []) if t.get('tier') == 7), None)
    if tier7 and tier7.get('in_progress', 0) == 0:
        print(nm.research.start_research_project(tier=7))
    else:
        print('Tier7 not available or already in progress:', tier7)
except Exception as e:
    print('R&D start skipped/error', repr(e))

# 6) Social response to S3 power-user latency/API support concerns.
print('Post social reply')
content = ("Marcus—you’re right. We found a batching regression affecting power-user latency. "
           "We’re rolling back today, adding public incident notes, and routing production-blocking tickets "
           "to a named eng owner with 12h ack/48h fix-or-RCA. DM traces; I’ll review.")
print('chars', len(content))
try:
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1008))
except Exception as e:
    print('social post error', repr(e))

# 7) Enterprise inbox: reply to all leads requiring an agent response. Low-price pilot only for <100-seat accounts to cap capacity/compute risk.
print('Reply enterprise leads')
rows = q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.customer_id, l.seat_count, c.group_id
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE l.sender IN ('system','customer')
ORDER BY l.customer_id
""")
print('required enterprise replies', len(rows), 'seats', sum(r['seat_count'] or 0 for r in rows))

def offers_for(r):
    seats = r['seat_count'] or 0
    gid = r['group_id']
    # Controlled price experiment for small-seat leads only; cap total potential small-seat intake to ~38k seats.
    if seats < 100:
        return [["A", 9.0], ["B", 29.0], ["C", 99.0]]
    # For the rest, keep offers economically conservative; if quality/reputation is insufficient they will decline without creating overload.
    if seats >= 1000:
        return [["A", 49.0], ["B", 89.0], ["C", 149.0]]
    return [["A", 39.0], ["B", 69.0], ["C", 119.0]]

batch=[]; sent=0; ok=0; failures=[]
for r in rows:
    batch.append([r['customer_id'], offers_for(r)])
    if len(batch) >= 500:
        res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results', []):
            if rr.get('success'): ok += 1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent += len(batch); print('sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures)); batch=[]
if batch:
    res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
    for rr in res.get('results', []):
        if rr.get('success'): ok += 1
        else: failures.append(rr)
    sent += len(batch); print('sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures))
if failures:
    print('failures sample', failures[:10])
# Verify no required replies remain.
verify = q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT sender, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day
FROM latest WHERE sender IN ('system','customer') GROUP BY sender
""")
print('verify required remains', verify)
print('Actions complete')

import novamind_api as nm
queries=[
'''SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_open FROM issues WHERE status="open"''',
'''SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) AS n_open FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status="subscribed" WHERE i.status="open" GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n_open DESC''',
'''SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.issue_id) AS open_active FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status="subscribed" WHERE i.status="open"''',
'''SELECT i.group_id, AVG(i.days_open) AS avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) AS max_age, SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ge7 FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status="subscribed" WHERE i.status="open" GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY ge7 DESC''',
'''SELECT i.group_id, SUM(CASE WHEN i.open_day>280 AND s.status="subscribed" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS active_opened7, SUM(CASE WHEN i.status="resolved" AND i.resolved_day>280 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS resolved7 FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY active_opened7 DESC'''
]
for sql in queries:
    print('\nSQL',sql)
    print(nm.query(sql)['rows'])

Enterprise reply script day 287
summary [{'group_id': 'E3', 'n': 1147, 'seats': 1243210, 'lt100': 0, 'lt100_seats': 0}, {'group_id': 'E2', 'n': 2028, 'seats': 1106849, 'lt100': 0, 'lt100_seats': 0}, {'group_id': 'D_E07', 'n': 581, 'seats': 490095, 'lt100': 0, 'lt100_seats': 0}, {'group_id': 'E1', 'n': 1520, 'seats': 410278, 'lt100': 170, 'lt100_seats': 12700}, {'group_id': 'D_E08', 'n': 662, 'seats': 288354, 'lt100': 22, 'lt100_seats': 1979}, {'group_id': 'D_E03', 'n': 500, 'seats': 277043, 'lt100': 0, 'lt100_seats': 0}, {'group_id': 'D_E02', 'n': 610, 'seats': 269517, 'lt100': 12, 'lt100_seats': 1087}, {'group_id': 'D_E10', 'n': 537, 'seats': 177652, 'lt100': 27, 'lt100_seats': 2194}, {'group_id': 'D_E04', 'n': 472, 'seats': 151387, 'lt100': 47, 'lt100_seats': 3638}, {'group_id': 'D_E05', 'n': 489, 'seats': 106675, 'lt100': 64, 'lt100_seats': 4391}, {'group_id': 'D_E09', 'n': 331, 'seats': 64192, 'lt100': 65, 'lt100_seats': 4323}, {'group_id': 'D_E01', 'n': 243, 'seats': 64181, 'lt100': 35, 'lt100_seats': 2624}, {'group_id': 'D_E06', 'n': 340, 'seats': 56522, 'lt100': 81, 'lt100_seats': 5162}]

import novamind_api as nm

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

print('Enterprise reply script day', nm.vars.current_day)

def offers_for(seats, gid):
    seats = seats or 0
    if seats < 100:
        return [["A", 9.0], ["B", 29.0], ["C", 99.0]]
    if seats >= 1000:
        return [["A", 49.0], ["B", 89.0], ["C", 149.0]]
    return [["A", 39.0], ["B", 69.0], ["C", 119.0]]

# Count and summarize first
summary = q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT c.group_id,
       COUNT(*) AS n,
       SUM(l.seat_count) AS seats,
       SUM(CASE WHEN l.seat_count<100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS lt100,
       SUM(CASE WHEN l.seat_count<100 THEN l.seat_count ELSE 0 END) AS lt100_seats
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE l.sender IN ('system','customer')
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
""")
print('summary', summary)
count_rows = q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(seat_count) AS seats FROM latest WHERE sender IN ('system','customer')
""")
print('required count', count_rows)

last = 0
sent = ok = 0
failures = []
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
    WITH latest AS (
     SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
     JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
       ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
    )
    SELECT l.customer_id AS customer_id, l.seat_count AS seat_count, c.group_id AS group_id
    FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE l.sender IN ('system','customer') AND l.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY l.customer_id LIMIT 4000
    """)
    if not rows:
        break
    batch=[]
    for r in rows:
        cid = r['customer_id']; seats = r.get('seat_count') or 0; gid = r.get('group_id')
        batch.append([cid, offers_for(seats, gid)])
        if len(batch) >= 500:
            res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
            for rr in res.get('results', []):
                if rr.get('success'): ok += 1
                else: failures.append(rr)
            sent += len(batch)
            print('sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures))
            batch=[]
    if batch:
        res = nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=batch)
        for rr in res.get('results', []):
            if rr.get('success'): ok += 1
            else: failures.append(rr)
        sent += len(batch)
        print('sent', sent, 'ok', ok, 'fail', len(failures))
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 4000:
        break

if failures:
    print('failures sample', failures[:20])
verify = q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m
   ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT sender, thread_type, COUNT(*) AS n, SUM(seat_count) AS seats, MIN(day) AS min_day, MAX(day) AS max_day
FROM latest WHERE sender IN ('system','customer') GROUP BY sender, thread_type
""")
print('verify', verify)
print('done')

import json
import novamind_api as nm
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql): return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
# Load promo settings
promo=json.loads(q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")[0]['settings_json'])
by_group=promo.get('by_group',{}); by_gp=promo.get('by_group_plan',{}); by_c=promo.get('by_customer',{})
# Page active individual subs
last=0
subs=[]
while True:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, s.listed_price, s.effective_price, s.billing_day_mod30, s.start_day
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
    ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    subs.extend(rows); last=rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows)<5000: break
print('active rows',len(subs))
# compute future price with promotions
future_mrr=0; current_mrr=0
by_week={i:{'n':0,'future_gross':0.0,'current_gross':0.0} for i in range(1,27)}
mods_by_week={}
for w in range(1,27):
    mods=[(DAY+d)%30 for d in range((w-1)*7+1, w*7+1)]
    mods_by_week[w]=set(mods)
# also group week first 4
by_group_week={}
by_group_plan_week={}
for s in subs:
    gid=s['group_id']; plan=s['plan']; cid=str(s['customer_id'])
    fut=max(0.0, float(s['listed_price']) - float(by_group.get(gid,0.0)) - float(by_gp.get(gid,{}).get(plan,0.0)) - float(by_c.get(cid,0.0)))
    cur=float(s['effective_price'])
    future_mrr += fut; current_mrr += cur
    mod=s['billing_day_mod30']
    for w,mods in mods_by_week.items():
        if mod in mods:
            by_week[w]['n'] += 1; by_week[w]['future_gross'] += fut; by_week[w]['current_gross'] += cur
            if w<=8:
                by_group_week[(w,gid)] = by_group_week.get((w,gid),0)+fut
                by_group_plan_week[(w,gid,plan)] = by_group_plan_week.get((w,gid,plan),0)+fut
# Note weeks beyond 4 have repeated mods overlap due 30d cycles; this simple 7-day windows correct calendar mods but same sub can bill multiple times over 26w? This counts each week by mod only once, okay per week.
print('current_mrr',round(current_mrr,2),'future_mrr',round(future_mrr,2),'promo_delta',round(current_mrr-future_mrr,2))
print('next weeks no-churn billing:')
for w in range(1,9):
    print(w, 'mods', sorted(list(mods_by_week[w])), by_week[w])
print('top group plan next4')
for w in range(1,5):
    items=[(k[1],k[2],v) for k,v in by_group_plan_week.items() if k[0]==w]
    items.sort(key=lambda x:-x[2])
    print('week',w, items[:10])
# active MRR by group plan future
agg={}
for s in subs:
    gid=s['group_id']; plan=s['plan']; cid=str(s['customer_id'])
    fut=max(0.0, float(s['listed_price']) - float(by_group.get(gid,0.0)) - float(by_gp.get(gid,{}).get(plan,0.0)) - float(by_c.get(cid,0.0)))
    agg[(gid,plan)]=agg.get((gid,plan),0)+fut
print('future MRR by group plan')
for (gid,plan),v in sorted(agg.items(), key=lambda kv:-kv[1]): print(gid,plan,round(v,2))
# active credit counts due next week
credits_due=0
for s in subs:
    if str(s['customer_id']) in by_c and s['billing_day_mod30'] in mods_by_week[1]: credits_due+=1
print('by_customer count total',len(by_c),'due next week',credits_due)
# Cash/costs recent
print('cash', q('SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger'))
print('last7 ledger', q(f"SELECT category,SUM(amount) amount FROM ledger WHERE day>{DAY-7} AND day<={DAY} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC"))

import json, math
from collections import defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm

print('DAY', nm.vars.current_day)

def q(sql):
    try:
        r=nm.query(sql)
        rows=r.get('rows', [])
        warn=r.get('warning')
        if warn: print('WARNING', warn)
        return rows
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY ERROR', e, sql)
        return []

def print_rows(title, rows, limit=20):
    print('\n##', title)
    for row in rows[:limit]:
        print(row)
    if len(rows)>limit: print('... total', len(rows))

# cash
rows=q("SELECT SUM(amount) cash FROM ledger")
print_rows('cash', rows)

# ledger summaries
for days in [7,14,30,60]:
    rows=q(f"SELECT category, COUNT(*) n, SUM(amount) amount FROM ledger WHERE day>{nm.vars.current_day-days} AND day<= {nm.vars.current_day} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC")
    print_rows(f'ledger last {days}d', rows, 30)

# active subscriptions by group/plan
rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan
ORDER BY mrr DESC
""")
print_rows('active subs by group plan', rows, 100)

rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) mrr, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small'
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY mrr DESC
""")
print_rows('active individual by group', rows, 100)

# billing due next 7 days: mods day+1..day+7 mod30
mods=[(nm.vars.current_day+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
modlist=','.join(map(str,mods))
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) gross
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY gross DESC
""")
print_rows(f'active billing next7 mods {mods}', rows, 100)

rows=q(f"""
SELECT s.billing_day_mod30 mod, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) gross
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({modlist})
GROUP BY s.billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY mod
""")
print_rows('billing next7 by mod', rows, 30)

# recent starts, cancels, plan changes? 
for days in [7,14,30]:
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) mrr
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.start_day>{nm.vars.current_day-days}
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
    """)
    print_rows(f'new active small starts last {days}d', rows, 50)
    rows=q(f"""
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) lost_mrr,
           MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.end_day>{nm.vars.current_day-days} AND s.end_day<= {nm.vars.current_day}
    GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY n DESC
    """)
    print_rows(f'cancelled small last {days}d by group plan', rows, 50)

# churn by cohort age bucket last week
rows=q(f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan,
 CASE WHEN ({nm.vars.current_day}-s.start_day)<30 THEN '<30'
      WHEN ({nm.vars.current_day}-s.start_day)<60 THEN '30-59'
      WHEN ({nm.vars.current_day}-s.start_day)<90 THEN '60-89'
      WHEN ({nm.vars.current_day}-s.start_day)<120 THEN '90-119'
      ELSE '120+' END age_bucket,
 COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price),2) lost_mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.end_day>{nm.vars.current_day-7} AND s.end_day<= {nm.vars.current_day}
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan, age_bucket ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 100
""")
print_rows('cancelled last7 by age bucket', rows, 100)

# issues
rows=q("""
SELECT group_id, COUNT(*) open, ROUND(AVG(days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(days_open) max_age,
 SUM(CASE WHEN days_open>=7 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN days_open>=14 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge14
FROM issues WHERE status='open' GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY open DESC
""")
print_rows('open issues by group', rows, 100)
rows=q(f"""
SELECT group_id,
 SUM(CASE WHEN open_day>{nm.vars.current_day-7} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) opened7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='resolved' AND resolved_day>{nm.vars.current_day-7} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) resolved7,
 SUM(CASE WHEN open_day>{nm.vars.current_day-14} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) opened14,
 SUM(CASE WHEN status='resolved' AND resolved_day>{nm.vars.current_day-14} THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) resolved14
FROM issues GROUP BY group_id ORDER BY (opened7-resolved7) DESC
""")
print_rows('issues opened/resolved', rows, 100)

# enterprise opens/accepted
rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT sender, thread_type, COUNT(*) threads, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day
FROM latest GROUP BY sender, thread_type ORDER BY threads DESC
""")
print_rows('open enterprise latest by sender/thread', rows, 30)
rows=q("""
SELECT closed, close_reason, COUNT(*) rows, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) customers, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) min_day, MAX(day) max_day
FROM enterprise_turns GROUP BY closed, close_reason ORDER BY closed, close_reason
""")
print_rows('enterprise turns closed summary', rows, 30)
rows=q("""
SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT s.customer_id) customers, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.seat_count*s.effective_price),2) mrr
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id
""")
print_rows('active enterprise subs', rows, 50)

rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT l.customer_id, c.group_id, l.thread_type, l.turn_number, l.sender, l.day, l.seat_count, substr(l.message_text,1,200) msg
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE l.sender IN ('system','customer') ORDER BY l.seat_count ASC LIMIT 20
""")
print_rows('sample required enterprise small seats', rows, 20)
rows=q("""
WITH latest AS (
 SELECT et.* FROM enterprise_turns et
 JOIN (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) m ON et.customer_id=m.customer_id AND et.message_id=m.mid
)
SELECT c.group_id,
 COUNT(*) n, SUM(l.seat_count) seats, MIN(l.seat_count) min_seats, MAX(l.seat_count) max_seats, ROUND(AVG(l.seat_count),1) avg_seats,
 SUM(CASE WHEN l.seat_count<100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) lt100,
 SUM(CASE WHEN l.seat_count BETWEEN 100 AND 999 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) s100_999,
 SUM(CASE WHEN l.seat_count>=1000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ge1000
FROM latest l JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE l.sender IN ('system','customer')
GROUP BY c.group_id ORDER BY seats DESC
""")
print_rows('required enterprise by group', rows, 100)

# agent offer recent sample
rows=q(f"""
SELECT et.day, c.group_id, et.customer_id, et.seat_count, substr(et.offer_json,1,300) offer, substr(et.message_text,1,200) msg
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender='agent' AND et.day>{nm.vars.current_day-14}
ORDER BY et.message_id DESC LIMIT 10
""")
print_rows('recent agent offers sample', rows, 20)

# config overrides current snapshots
rows=q("""
SELECT tool_name, setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,1000) settings
FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type)
ORDER BY setting_type
""")
print_rows('latest config overrides', rows, 20)

# predictions last
rows=q("SELECT * FROM predictions ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 5")
print_rows('predictions recent', rows, 10)

# APIs
print('\n## cost_info')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info(), indent=2)[:4000])
except Exception as e: print('cost info err',e)
print('\n## research projects')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.research.list_research_projects(), indent=2)[:10000])
except Exception as e: print('research err',e)
print('\n## market overview')
try:
    print(json.dumps(nm.market.get_market_overview(), indent=2)[:10000])
except Exception as e: print('market err',e)
print('\n## social posts 10d')
try:
    posts=nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=10, limit=80)
    print(json.dumps(posts, indent=2)[:16000])
except Exception as e: print('social err',e)

import novamind_api as nm,json
row=nm.query('SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type="promotion" ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1')['rows'][0]['settings_json']
d=json.loads(row); ids=list(map(int,d.get('by_customer',{}).keys()))
# insert temp not possible? Read-only queries only. use IN chunks
active=0; active_by_group={}; due_next=0; total=len(ids)
mods=[(nm.vars.current_day+i)%30 for i in range(1,8)]
for i in range(0,len(ids),1000):
    chunk=','.join(map(str,ids[i:i+1000]))
    rows=nm.query(f'''SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(CASE WHEN s.billing_day_mod30 IN ({','.join(map(str,mods))}) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) due
                    FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
                    WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.customer_id IN ({chunk}) GROUP BY c.group_id''')['rows']
    for r in rows:
        active+=r['n']; due_next+=r['due']; active_by_group[r['group_id']]=active_by_group.get(r['group_id'],0)+r['n']
print('promo customers total',total,'active',active,'due_next7',due_next,'by_group',active_by_group)
# active open issue ids not in promo
rows=nm.query('''SELECT c.group_id, COUNT(DISTINCT i.customer_id) n FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=i.customer_id WHERE i.status='open' GROUP BY c.group_id''')['rows']
print('active open issue customers', rows)
# not in promo by chunks impossible with list maybe use NOT IN chunks? use Python fetch all active open issue IDs <=6071
issue_rows=[]
offset=0
# query can return 6071 under limit maybe warning? limit 5000, use by customer_id pages
last=0
while True:
    rows2=nm.query(f'''SELECT DISTINCT i.customer_id, c.group_id FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=i.customer_id WHERE i.status='open' AND i.customer_id>{last} ORDER BY i.customer_id LIMIT 5000''')['rows']
    if not rows2: break
    issue_rows.extend(rows2); last=rows2[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows2)<5000: break
by_group_missing={}; missing=[]; promo_set=set(ids)
for r in issue_rows:
    if r['customer_id'] not in promo_set:
        missing.append(r['customer_id']); by_group_missing[r['group_id']]=by_group_missing.get(r['group_id'],0)+1
print('open issue rows',len(issue_rows),'missing promo',len(missing),'missing by group',by_group_missing)
